race.name: La Savoyarde
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers of freestyle racing across the snow-covered firs and sunny plateaus of Savoie Grand Revard — France's Marathon Ski Tour at its alpine finest.

vitals.registration: Online registration via Marathon Ski Tour

climate.description: February on the Savoie Grand Revard plateau at 1,400m means reliable snow cover and moderate Alpine cold. Temperatures range from -10°C to 0°C. The plateau's exposure to sun creates pleasant racing conditions but can soften snow on south-facing sections.
challenges: Altitude effects at 1,400m — moderate performance impact
Sun exposure on the open plateau can affect wax and snow conditions
Variable snow quality between shaded forest and open terrain

course.primary: A 42km freestyle course across the Savoie Grand Revard plateau, renowned for its snow-covered fir forests and sunny slopes. The course combines forested sections with open plateau skiing through one of France's most celebrated Nordic areas.
course.surface: Machine-groomed Nordic trails through alpine plateau and forest
course.grooming: Well-maintained La Feclaz Nordic center trails
features: Savoie Grand Revard plateau — one of France's premier Nordic ski areas
Snow-covered fir forests and sunny open plateaus
Part of the Marathon Ski Tour circuit
Stunning views of the Chartreuse and Bauges mountain ranges

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A solid Marathon Ski Tour and Ski Classics Challengers event on the scenic Savoie Grand Revard plateau. Moderate field size and primarily French participation limit international draw and competitive depth.

history.summary: La Savoyarde is an established international Nordic skiing marathon held at La Feclaz on the Savoie Grand Revard plateau. The race combines cross-country skiing competition with a festival atmosphere, attracting recreational skiers, gliding enthusiasts, and serious competitors. It is part of both the Marathon Ski Tour circuit and Ski Classics Challengers calendar, giving it visibility beyond France's borders.
notable_facts: Part of the Marathon Ski Tour — France's premier XC ski marathon circuit
Ski Classics Challengers event — connects to the international pro racing calendar
Held on the Savoie Grand Revard plateau, one of France's most scenic Nordic areas
Combines competitive racing with recreational participation
La Feclaz is one of the French Alps' premier Nordic ski centers
2026 winners: Victor Cullet Calderini (men) and Camille Coupe (women)
